summary: Iteration 12 - Post-refactor regression testing (server.py 3518 → 109 lines). Backend: 40/40 tests passed (100%). Frontend: Dashboard and Market Intel pages fully working. Risk Management page has 3 broken sections due to API field name regressions introduced during refactor.

ui_bugs.issue: Shows 'undefined%' for Current Drawdown and Threshold. Root cause: refactored /api/risk/circuit-breaker returns `current_drawdown_pct` and `max_drawdown_limit` but RiskPage.js uses `cb.current_drawdown` and `cb.max_drawdown_threshold`

Shows only 'OUTSIDE' badge with no session cards and no UTC time. Root cause: refactored /api/risk/sessions returns `session_ok`, `current_session`, `all_sessions`, `allowed_sessions`, `current_hour_utc` but RiskPage.js uses `in_session`, `active_session`, `sessions`, `allowed`, `current_utc`

Section is completely empty (no symbol cards). Root cause: refactored /api/risk/regime returns top-level `{symbol, regime, strength, details, all_symbols: {BTCUSDT: 'TRENDING_UP' (string)}}` but RiskPage.js uses `regimes?.regimes` which doesn't exist; also `all_symbols` contains strings not objects with regime/strength/details properties

issues: Pre-existing: Console error '<tr> cannot be child of <span>' hydration issue (cosmetic, same as iterations 10-11)

action_items: FIX CRITICAL: /api/risk/circuit-breaker field names - change `current_drawdown_pct` back to `current_drawdown` and `max_drawdown_limit` back to `max_drawdown_threshold` in routes/risk_routes.py (OR update RiskPage.js to use new field names)
FIX CRITICAL: /api/risk/sessions field names - change back to `in_session`, `active_session`, `sessions`, `allowed`, `current_utc` (OR update RiskPage.js)
FIX CRITICAL: /api/risk/regime response structure - change back to return `{regimes: {BTCUSDT: {regime, strength, details, price}, ...}}` format (OR update RiskPage.js)
critical_code_review_comments: The backend refactor introduced 3 API field name regressions in risk_routes.py that broke the Risk Management page. The legacy server used: circuit-breaker: {current_drawdown, max_drawdown_threshold}, sessions: {in_session, active_session, sessions, allowed, current_utc, current_hour}, regime: {regimes: {symbol: {regime, strength, details}}}. The refactored version renamed all these fields without updating the frontend.

The /api/risk/regime response structure also changed - the new `all_symbols` dict contains only regime strings (not objects with regime/strength/details) unlike the old `regimes` dict which had full objects per symbol

success_rate.frontend: 67% (Dashboard ✓, Market Intel ✓, Risk Management 1/4 sections working - Monte Carlo ✓, Circuit Breaker ✗, Sessions ✗, Market Regime ✗)

context_for_next_testing_agent: Regression test after major refactor (server.py 3518→109 lines). All 40 backend APIs pass. Risk Management page has 3 broken sections due to API field name mismatches introduced in the refactor. The fixes are straightforward: either update routes/risk_routes.py to match old field names or update RiskPage.js to use new field names. The new refactored backend correctly routes all endpoints. Binance is geo-restricted - all market data is simulated. Test user: regression_test@example.com / testpass123

risk_page_broken.root_cause: During the server.py refactoring (3518→109 lines), the risk_routes.py module was written with different field names than the original server.py implementation. The frontend (RiskPage.js) was not updated to match.

regime.refactored_returns: { symbol, regime, strength, details, all_symbols: {BTCUSDT: 'TRENDING_UP'} (strings only) }

risk_page_broken.fix_recommendation: Update routes/risk_routes.py to use the same field names as the legacy implementation OR update RiskPage.js to use the new field names from the refactored backend

mocked_api_note: All /api/orderflow, /api/funding-rates, /api/whale-activity return SIMULATED data. Binance is geo-restricted in this environment. This is expected and not a bug.
